LanguageData.LetterLabelZombielandMapSetupFailed: Zombieland did not start on this map
LanguageData.ZombielandMapSetupFailed:
RimWorld loaded this map, but Zombieland could not finish its map setup. Zombieland tried to repair the problem and could not.

What this means: zombies will not spawn or move on this map. Zombie events, zombie avoidance, zombie corpses, and special zombie behavior may not work here.

This is usually caused by an error while RimWorld or another mod was loading the map. Check the first red error earlier in the log, then try disabling recently added or updated mods. Also make sure RimWorld and Zombieland are compatible versions.
LanguageData.ZombielandPlayerLogRevealFailed: Could not reveal Player.log. The path was copied to the clipboard.
LanguageData.ZombielandPlayerLogPathUnavailable: Zombieland could not find the Player.log path.
LanguageData.Zombieland_Settings: Hover over any element to read a help text about it to the right. All settings can be changed in-game and are active immediately, they are saved with the game.\n\nTo set defaults for new games, change the settings from the main screen (Options/Mod Settings/Zombieland).
LanguageData.ZombieLandKeyframes: You can add keyframes that define settings in time. Use the slider to check the values in-between. Only settings for keyframes can be edited.
LanguageData.ZombieLandKeyframe0: <- first keyframe (start of the game) . . . future game time ->
LanguageData.Difficulty_ZombielandThreatScale_Info: Controls the difficulty of almost all things in Zombieland, like zombie abilities, threat forecast, and much more.
LanguageData.ZombiesRisingNearYourBase: A group of zombies appears near your base
LanguageData.ZombiesSpitter: A zombie spitter has entered the map and will lay siege to you
LanguageData.ZombieSymbiant: A Zombie Symbiant has surfaced in {0} and linked itself to {1}. It is disruptive, not an immediate killer: it spreads through used rooms, slows pawns that cross its slime, lowers room quality, and strengthens the host as it grows. Attacks first drain its damage guard; violent guard failure kills the host. Leaving its room open only makes a linked root move into another used room. Feed it with a humanlike corpse or coagulant pack to restore the guard, build surgery reserve, and shrink it; when the inspect pane says surgery is ready, operate on {1}. Failed surgery can injure the host; killing the host ends the bond at the cost of the colonist.
LanguageData.ZombieSymbiantNoHost: A Zombie Symbiant has surfaced in {0}. It is disruptive, not an immediate killer: it spreads through used rooms, slows pawns that cross its slime, and lowers room quality. Attacks first drain its damage guard. Feed it with a humanlike corpse or coagulant pack to restore the guard, build surgery reserve, and shrink it.
LanguageData.SymbiantGrowthUprooted: uprooted; waiting for a stable room
LanguageData.SymbiantGrowthRelocating: moving old slime into used rooms
LanguageData.SymbiantGrowthDormantNoRoom: dormant; waiting for a used indoor room
LanguageData.SymbiantGrowthContained: contained; no used indoor room reachable
LanguageData.SymbiantSeveranceNeedsMaturity: needs more time in used rooms
LanguageData.SymbiantSeveranceNeedsReserve: feed it to fill surgery reserve
LanguageData.SymbiantSeveranceNeedsShrink: feed it down to 3 cells or fewer
LanguageData.SymbiantWeaponRejectedMessage: The Symbiant damage guard absorbed the hit. Guard: {0}/{1}. Feeding restores the guard, builds surgery reserve, and shrinks the visible mass.
LanguageData.SymbiantDamageGuardBrokenMessage: The Symbiant damage guard ruptured. {0}'s bond collapsed violently.
LanguageData.FeedZombieSymbiantDesc: Marks the Symbiant for feeding. Colonists will bring a humanlike corpse or a Symbiant coagulant pack. Feeding restores the damage guard, builds surgery reserve, can shrink the visible mass, pauses growth, and cannot remove the final cells. When surgery is ready, operate on the linked host.
LanguageData.NoSymbiantFeed: No humanlike corpse or Symbiant coagulant pack available
LanguageData.SymbiantHostBondDescription: Bond: {0}/{1} cells, {2}% host benefit, surgery reserve {3}/{4}. {5}
LanguageData.SymbiantHostBondNotReady: Unsafe surgery: feed the Symbiant until reserve is full and visible size is {0} cells or fewer.
LanguageData.SymbiantHostBondMissing: No active Symbiant is linked to this host.
LanguageData.SymbiantHostRelocatedMessage: {0}'s Symbiant bond went dormant after leaving the map. The remaining hostless slime can be fed away.
LanguageData.SymbiantReseededMessage: {0}'s Symbiant root moved into another used room after its chamber stayed open. Old slime is moving back into the new root; feeding and surgery remain the reliable way to end the bond.
LanguageData.XDaysUntilZombies: Zombies will appear where there is soft soil, dirt, sand, or gravel. Building floors prevents zombies from emerging from those cells.
LanguageData.ZombielandRestartRequired: Changing this setting requires a restart of RimWorld
LanguageData.ZombieBiteInfectionChance: Risk that a zombie bite is infectious
LanguageData.AnyTreatmentStopsInfection: Treatment in a bed stops infections
LanguageData.HoursAfterDeathToBecomeZombie: Hours to become a zombie after death
LanguageData.DeadBecomesZombieMessage: Show a message when someone turns into a zombie
LanguageData.DangerousSituationMessage: Show a message when in danger
LanguageData.LootExtractAmount: Amount of loot that zombies drop
LanguageData.RagingZombies: Zombies rage if a group gets too large
LanguageData.WallPushing: Number of zombies that will push other zombies over walls
LanguageData.SymbiantFullBenefitRoomCoverage: Room coverage for full Symbiant bond
LanguageData.Zombieland_Twinkie_Description: A Twinkie is an American snack cake, marketed as a "Golden Sponge Cake with Creamy Filling".
LanguageData.PlayCreepyAmbientSound: Play creepy ambient sound at night
LanguageData.ConfirmUninstallZombieland: If you continue, you can choose a new save file name and you will return to the main menu after the game is saved. The new save can be loaded without Zombieland.\n\nDo you really want to remove all Zombieland content from the current game?
LanguageData.ActivateDescription: Activates the device. Zombies in the room lose consciousness.
LanguageData.WrongPlacementZombieShocker: The device cannot be placed here.
LanguageData.ZombiesAreBeingPushedOverYourWalls: There are zombies being pushed over your walls! Prevent zombies from forming dense groups.
LanguageData.ColonistBecameAZombieLabel: A colonist turned into a zombie
LanguageData.BodyIsInfectedLabel: This body is infected with the zombie virus!
LanguageData.BodyIsInfectedTooltip: Infected bodies will turn into zombies when they start to rot.
LanguageData.AutoExtractAllowedDescription: Automatically harvest zombie extract when no zombies are nearby
LanguageData.AutoExtractForbiddenDescription: Only harvest zombie extract when ordered
LanguageData.AutoDoubleTapAllowedDescription: Automatically double tap corpses to prevent them from becoming zombies
LanguageData.AutoAvoidZombiesEnabledDescription: Automatically avoid zombies, except while drafted
LanguageData.AutoAvoidZombiesDisabledDescription: Ignore zombies. You need to manually control all actions
LanguageData.ZombieShockerWrongPlacement: The device cannot be placed here
LanguageData.ZombieShockerHasNoBattery: The device has no battery attached
LanguageData.ZombieShockerHasNoRoom: The device has no valid room attached
LanguageData.ZombieShockerLowBatteryState: The device has depleted the attached batteries
LanguageData.ZombieRiskAreaColumnHintLong: Zombieland area rules: violations appear at the top of the screen during play
LanguageData.ZombieRiskAreaColumnHintMedium: Zombieland violations appear at the top
LanguageData.DisableRandomApparel: Turn off random apparel for zombies
LanguageData.ChainsawDisabled: Chainsaw cannot be used right now
LanguageData.ChainsawTurnOn: Turn chainsaw on to attack any nearby zombies
LanguageData.ChainsawTurnOff: Turn chainsaw off to conserve fuel
LanguageData.ThumperControlDesc: Define intensity and interval for this zombie thumper
LanguageData.ToggleStateDesc: Turns the device on or off.
LanguageData.ContaminationHelp: Zombie contamination is transferred by touch and by handling contaminated things. It spreads around and can lead to strange behavior in colonists.
LanguageData.ShowContaminationOverlayToggleButton: Toggle zombie contamination overlay.\n\nThis shows how contaminated things and the ground are, which in turn affects pawns.
